api/client/javascript/src/react.purpose: Thin React integration layer for the OpenMeter JavaScript SDK — provides a context/provider/hook trio so React apps can inject an OpenMeter client instance and consume it anywhere in the component tree. This is the only file in the folder; all SDK logic lives in ../portal/index.js which is re-exported wholesale.

patterns.name: Re-export portal surface
patterns.description: The file does `export * from '../portal/index.js'` so all portal SDK types/functions are available from the react import path without duplication.

patterns.name: Null-initialized context
patterns.description: OpenMeterContext is created with `null` as default; useOpenMeter throws if context is `undefined` (uninitialised, not null). Callers must check for null (unauthenticated) themselves.

patterns.name: 'use client' directive
patterns.description: File is marked 'use client' at the top — required for Next.js App Router compatibility. Any addition to this file must preserve that directive.

key_file_guides.role: Entire folder is this single file. Exports OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, useOpenMeter, and all portal SDK symbols.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here. All SDK behaviour belongs in ../portal/. Adding new exports here should come from ../portal/ via re-export, not defined locally.

anti_patterns: Defining SDK methods or API call logic directly in this file instead of delegating to ../portal/
Removing the 'use client' directive — breaks Next.js App Router
Creating a second context or second provider for different API concerns — extend OpenMeter type in portal instead
Throwing inside useOpenMeter when value is null (currently it only throws on undefined — null is a valid 'not logged in' state)

decisions.decision: Context default is null, not a noop object
decisions.rationale: Forces callers to handle the unauthenticated state explicitly rather than silently calling noop methods.

decisions.decision: All portal exports are re-exported from this entry point
decisions.rationale: Consumers importing from the react path get the full SDK surface without a second import statement, keeping usage ergonomic.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap app with provider and consume in a component

api/client/javascript/src/zod.purpose: Generated Zod validation schemas for every OpenMeter Cloud API request/response shape. Produced by orval v8.7.0 from the OpenAPI spec — never edited manually. Provides runtime type-safety and coercion on top of the generated TypeScript types.

patterns.name: Named constraint constants before schema
patterns.description: Every regex, min, max, and default value is extracted as a named `export const` (e.g. `listAddonsQueryPageSizeMax = 1000`) before the schema object. Schemas reference these constants, never inline literals.

patterns.name: zod.coerce for primitives
patterns.description: All primitive fields use `zod.coerce.string()` / `zod.coerce.number()` / `zod.coerce.boolean()` to tolerate string-serialised query params from HTTP.

patterns.name: zod.union for discriminated request bodies
patterns.description: Rate card and entitlement template variants use `zod.union([...])` with fully inlined object schemas — no shared sub-schema references within the file.

patterns.name: Generated file — no manual edits
patterns.description: Header `Generated by orval v8.7.0 🍺 Do not edit manually.` is authoritative. Regenerate via `make gen-api`.
patterns.example:
// Generated by orval v8.7.0 🍺
// Do not edit manually.

key_file_guides.role: Single generated file exporting all Zod schemas and constraint constants for every API operation. Used by client-side form validation and server-side request parsing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: File is truncated in the provided view — it covers the full OpenAPI surface (plans, subscriptions, billing, etc.) beyond the addon excerpt shown. Never reference or import specific line ranges.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing index.ts — all changes must come from regenerating via `make gen-api` from the TypeSpec source
Importing this file in Go server code — it is TypeScript only
Adding custom validators outside index.ts in this folder — extend in the consuming application instead
Relying on specific constant names in application code without re-exporting them, as names are derived from operation/parameter names which change with the spec

decisions.decision: orval generates Zod schemas from OpenAPI rather than hand-authoring
decisions.rationale: Keeps runtime validation in sync with the TypeSpec contract automatically; drift is impossible when make gen-api runs both steps.

decisions.decision: Constraint values extracted as named exports
decisions.rationale: Allows consuming code to import and reference the same boundary values (e.g. max page size) that the schema enforces, without duplicating magic numbers.
